items.title: How much water is there on Earth?

image.alt: A 3D image of the Earth compared to different spheres and cubes representing different pools of water. Four different sized water-spheres from largest to smallest represent the total water, oceanic water, groundwater, and lake and river water on Earth. An ice-cube between oceanic water and ground water represents the total amount of frozen water on Earth. The size of the spheres and cubes are realistically scaled to directly compare each pool's volume on Earth.
A bivariate map of the western U.S. showing the relationship between snow water equivalent and historic snowfall
